title: Korea
subtitle: 12 days · Jeju Fantasy Path || Busan to Seoul
body:
A special one — Adrien joined me for my South Korean leg of my Tour around Asia!

Over the course of 5 days, we rode the length of South Korea starting in Busan and ending in Seoul, following the 4 Rivers Cycling Path the entire way.

The route was approximately 380 miles (600+ kms) with an average of 75 miles each day. Aside from a mountain getting in the way, the entirety of the route is very flat with only an occasional steep hill.

After 2 months of solo touring the length of Japan, it was an excellent change of pace to have company on this stint of my journey. Thanks for flying out, Adrien!

Field Notes:
· Cycling is very popular in Korea, but primarily as a form of recreation and not so much as a means of transportation
· There were plenty of spots to camp, but showers and facilities were hard to come by
· The usual rest stops were had at the convenience stores that pepper the peninsula — CU, GS25, 7-Eleven, eMart, etc.
· Koreans love Park Golf as a form of leisurely activity, often pursued in the early mornings and evenings when the sun is not nearly as intense
· On our last night, met a Russian refugee who had expatriated to Korea to escape the draft to fight in Ukraine
· Adrien experienced one major mechanical when his bike tipped over causing the valve on his wheel to snap in half, which later resulted in the bike also coming out of true
· Along the way, we passed various dams constructed on the rivers, each with a very unique design and style
· Passport stamps collected: 15? Maybe?
